task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly calculates the trimmed mean of a list after removing one occurrence of the minimum and maximum, handling edge cases such as an empty input list.
trace:
First, I sorted the list in ascending order to find the minimum and maximum values easily. Then, I identified the indices of the minimum and maximum values (excluding them from the calculation). Since the number of elements in the trimmed section may be odd or even, I handled both cases separately by calculating the mean accordingly. Lastly, I returned the calculated mean as a float.

The edge case I addressed is when the input list is empty; in this situation, the function returns None instead of raising an error.
